abstract: Constants for the tag schemes specified when initializing a linguistic tagger.

codeExamples.code:
let tagger = NLTagger(tagSchemes: [.lexicalClass, .language, .script], options: 0)
tagger.string = "Überraschung"

tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .lexicalClass, tokenRange: nil) // Noun
tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .language, tokenRange: nil) // german
tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .script, tokenRange: nil) // Latn

overview:
## Overview

When initializing a linguistic tagger with [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/init(_:)], you specify one or more tag schemes that correspond to the kind of information you’re interested in for a selection of natural language text. To ensure optimal performance, avoid specifying tag schemes that you won’t use.

Some tag schemes are only available for certain units and languages. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger/availableTagSchemes(for:language:)] to determine the possible values for a specified language and linguistic unit.

When working with linguistic tags using the methods described in Getting linguistic tags and Enumerating linguistic tags in [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger], the returned tag value depends on the specified scheme. For example, given the token “Überraschung”, the returned tag is [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTag/noun] when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/lexicalClass] tag scheme, [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLLanguage/german] (German language) when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/language] tag scheme, and “Latn” (Latin script) when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/script] tag scheme, as shown in the following code.

source: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme
crawled: 2026-03-10T20:12:25Z
---

# NLTagScheme

**Structure**

Constants for the tag schemes specified when initializing a linguistic tagger.

## Declaration

```swift
struct NLTagScheme
```

## Overview

When initializing a linguistic tagger with [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/init(_:)], you specify one or more tag schemes that correspond to the kind of information you’re interested in for a selection of natural language text. To ensure optimal performance, avoid specifying tag schemes that you won’t use.

Some tag schemes are only available for certain units and languages. Use [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger/availableTagSchemes(for:language:)] to determine the possible values for a specified language and linguistic unit.

When working with linguistic tags using the methods described in Getting linguistic tags and Enumerating linguistic tags in [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagger], the returned tag value depends on the specified scheme. For example, given the token “Überraschung”, the returned tag is [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTag/noun] when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/lexicalClass] tag scheme, [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLLanguage/german] (German language) when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/language] tag scheme, and “Latn” (Latin script) when using the [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/script] tag scheme, as shown in the following code.

```swift
let tagger = NLTagger(tagSchemes: [.lexicalClass, .language, .script], options: 0)
tagger.string = "Überraschung"

tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .lexicalClass, tokenRange: nil) // Noun
tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .language, tokenRange: nil) // german
tagger.tag(at: 0, unit: .word, scheme: .script, tokenRange: nil) // Latn
```

## Schemes

- **tokenType**: A scheme that classifies tokens according to their broad type: word, punctuation, or whitespace.
- **lexicalClass**: A scheme that classifies tokens according to class: part of speech, type of punctuation, or whitespace.
- **nameType**: A scheme that classifies tokens according to whether they are part of a named entity.
- **nameTypeOrLexicalClass**: A scheme that classifies tokens corresponding to names according to [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/nameType], and classifies all other tokens according to [doc://com.apple.naturallanguage/documentation/NaturalLanguage/NLTagScheme/lexicalClass].
- **lemma**: A scheme that supplies a stem form of a word token, if known.
- **language**: A scheme that supplies the language for a token, if it can determine one.
- **script**: A scheme that supplies the script for a token, if it can determine one.
- **sentimentScore**: A scheme that scores text as positive, negative, or neutral based on its sentiment polarity.

## Initializers

- **init(_:)**: Creates a tag scheme.
- **init(rawValue:)**: Creates a tag scheme with the specified raw value.

## Getting the tag schemes

- **availableTagSchemes(for:language:)**: Retrieves the tag schemes available for a particular unit (like word or sentence) and language on the current device.
- **requestAssets(for:tagScheme:completionHandler:)**: Asks the Natural Language framework to load any missing assets for a tag scheme onto the device for the given language.
- **NLTagger.AssetsResult**: The response to an asset request.
- **tagSchemes**: The tag schemes configured for this linguistic tagger.

## Conforms To

- Equatable
- Hashable
- RawRepresentable
- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
